title: Explain Settings
short_answer: Settings are the app knobs that control how ASSISCLUE starts and behaves.
text: Settings live in app/settings and hold defaults for audio, UI, persona, LLM, TTS, and app mode. Think of them like safe switches you can inspect before changing app behavior.
more: The settings folder is app/settings. The final app config is built in app/config.py by AppConfig.build(...), which mirrors play defaults into system mode, persona, and UI config. If you change defaults, edit the specific settings file instead of changing random runtime files.

title: Explain App Settings
text: app_settings.py controls broad app behavior like offline mode, private mode, tools, web search, cleanup days, and which UI panels are visible. Use it for general app-wide defaults.
more: The file is app/settings/app_settings.py and it defines the AppSettings dataclass. Important fields include system_mode, enable_background_capture, enable_tools, enable_web_search, runtime_cleanup_days, and show_*_panel flags. Be careful with web search and tool switches because they can change what the assistant is allowed to do.

title: Explain Play Settings
short_answer: play_settings.py controls what main loops and defaults start with the app.
text: play_settings.py decides if UI, assistant loop, STT loop, and screenshot loop start. It also stores default persona, default system mode, UI host, UI port, and loop timing.
more: The file is app/settings/play_settings.py and it defines PlaySettings. AppConfig.build(...) copies default_system_mode, default_persona, ui_host, ui_port, and ui_reload from PlaySettings into the global AppConfig. Change this file when startup defaults are wrong, not when only one runtime session needs a temporary value.

title: Explain Audio Settings
short_answer: audio_settings.py controls microphone, live STT, wake word, and TTS playback.
text: Audio settings are for voice input and speaker output. Use them when the app hears the wrong device, misses speech, catches too much noise, or speaks through the wrong output.
more: The file is app/settings/audio_settings.py and it defines AudioSettings. It includes wake_word, stt_live_* capture tunables, Windows device names, tts_playback_enabled, tts_output_device_name, and tts_output_gain. Change device names carefully and prefer stable short substrings such as a known microphone name.

title: Explain LLM Settings
short_answer: llm_settings.py controls the LLM provider, models, limits, and timeouts.
text: LLM settings tell the app which model server and model names to use. Use them when changing Ollama, text model, vision model, answer size, or timeout behavior.
more: The file is app/settings/llm_settings.py and it defines LLMSettings. It reads ENV keys like LLM_PROVIDER, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, OLLAMA_HOST, LLM_TEXT_MODEL, LLM_VISION_MODEL, LLM_MAX_TOKENS, and LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS. The code keeps the vision model separate from the text model so image analysis does not accidentally use a text-only model.

title: Explain TTS Settings
short_answer: tts_settings.py controls the text-to-speech backend, voice, language, and sample rate.
text: TTS settings are for generated assistant speech. Use them when changing Kokoro voice, TTS language code, backend, or audio sample rate.
more: The file is app/settings/tts_settings.py and it defines TTSSettings. It reads ASSISCLUE_TTS_BACKEND, ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_LANG, ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_VOICE, and ASSISCLUE_TTS_SAMPLE_RATE from ENV, with Kokoro defaults. Speaker device and playback gain are in app/settings/audio_settings.py, not tts_settings.py.

title: Explain AppConfig
short_answer: AppConfig is the final config object the app builds from settings files.
text: AppConfig gathers the settings into one place so the app can read clean final values. It also copies play defaults into active startup values.
more: The file is app/config.py. AppConfig.build(project_root) creates PathsConfig, AppSettings, AudioSettings, PlaySettings, TTSSettings, PersonaConfig, ModelConfig, MemoryConfig, UIConfig, and ScreenshotConfig. It mirrors play.default_system_mode, play.default_persona, play.ui_host, play.ui_port, and play.ui_reload into the global config.

title: Explain ENV
short_answer: ENV values let you change some settings without editing Python files.
text: ENV is useful for machine-specific choices like Ollama URL, model names, TTS backend, TTS voice, and TTS sample rate. Use ENV when different computers need different values.
more: The README lists common ENV keys: LLM_PROVIDER, OLLAMA_BASE_URL, OLLAMA_HOST, LLM_TEXT_MODEL, LLM_VISION_MODEL, ASSISCLUE_TTS_BACKEND, ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_LANG, ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_VOICE, and ASSISCLUE_TTS_SAMPLE_RATE. These are read mainly in app/settings/llm_settings.py and app/settings/tts_settings.py. If no ENV is set, the dataclass defaults are used.

title: Explain System Mode
short_answer: System Mode describes how active the assistant should be.
text: System mode is the app's activity level, such as passive or listening_light. It helps describe whether the assistant should stay quiet or be more active.
more: app/settings/app_settings.py documents modes as passive, listening_light, smart_background, and fully_active. app/settings/play_settings.py sets default_system_mode to listening_light, and app/config.py copies that value into config.app.system_mode during AppConfig.build(...). Check code behavior before assuming every service fully obeys every mode.

title: Change UI Port
short_answer: UI Port controls where the local web app opens.
text: Use UI Port when another app is already using the default local web port. The default port is 8000 on host 127.0.0.1.
more: The UI host, port, and reload values live in app/settings/play_settings.py as ui_host, ui_port, and ui_reload. AppConfig.build(...) copies them into config.ui. Keep ui_host as 127.0.0.1 for local-only use unless you intentionally want network access.

title: Change Wake Word
short_answer: Wake Word is the phrase the assistant listens for before reacting.
text: The wake word helps the voice system know when a spoken phrase is meant for the assistant. Keep it short and clear so live STT can match it reliably.
more: The wake word lives in app/settings/audio_settings.py as wake_word, currently defaulting to hey assistant. It works near the live STT and mic settings, so if triggering is unreliable, also inspect stt_live_* quality and silence settings. Avoid long or noisy phrases because speech recognition may miss them.

title: Explain Default Persona
short_answer: Default Persona is the persona chosen when the app starts.
text: Default persona controls which assistant personality starts first. In this app, the default is rick unless another runtime choice overrides it.
more: The default lives in app/settings/play_settings.py as default_persona. app/config.py copies it into config.persona.active_persona during AppConfig.build(...). The persona profiles themselves live under app/personas/profiles, so change the default name only to a persona that actually exists.

title: Tune STT Live
short_answer: STT Live settings control live speech-to-text capture.
text: STT Live is the live listening part for voice input. Tune it when speech is too slow, clipped, too quiet, or polluted by background noise.
more: Live STT knobs live in app/settings/audio_settings.py as stt_live_* fields. Useful fields include stt_live_chunk_seconds, stt_live_chunk_overlap_seconds, stt_live_input_gain, stt_live_min_confidence, and voice peak thresholds. Raise quality gates to block noise, or lower them carefully if real short phrases are ignored.

title: Change TTS Voice
short_answer: TTS Voice controls the generated voice used for assistant speech.
text: Use TTS Voice when you want the assistant to speak with a different generated voice. This changes the voice model choice, not the speaker device.
more: The TTS voice is read in app/settings/tts_settings.py from ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_VOICE, defaulting to af_heart. The language code comes from ASSISCLUE_KOKORO_LANG and sample rate from ASSISCLUE_TTS_SAMPLE_RATE. If audio plays through the wrong speaker, check tts_output_device_name in app/settings/audio_settings.py instead.

title: Change Ollama URL
short_answer: Ollama URL tells the app where the local Ollama model server is.
text: Use Ollama URL when Ollama is running on another host or port. By default, the app uses the local Ollama server at 127.0.0.1:11434.
more: app/settings/llm_settings.py resolves the base URL from OLLAMA_BASE_URL first, then OLLAMA_HOST, then http://127.0.0.1:11434. The helper normalizes missing http:// and removes trailing slashes. If models do not respond, check this URL and then verify the model names in LLM_TEXT_MODEL and LLM_VISION_MODEL.

title: Change Settings Defaults
short_answer: Change settings defaults by editing the right .py file in app/settings.
text: Use this when you want a default value to change without using ENV. Pick the settings file that owns the value, then change only that default.
more: The README says the main config is built through AppConfig.build(...) and the settings files live in app/settings. For non-ENV defaults, edit the matching .py file: app_settings.py for general switches, audio_settings.py for mic/STT/playback, play_settings.py for startup loops and UI/persona defaults, tts_settings.py for TTS ENV defaults, and llm_settings.py for LLM ENV defaults. Do not change runtime files when you mean to change a startup default.
